findings.title: Missing optional manifest metadata reduces transparency
findings.description: The skill omits optional manifest fields such as `allowed-tools`, `license`, and `compatibility`. While not inherently malicious, the absence of these fields reduces clarity about intended permissions, operational scope, and reuse constraints, making security review and safe execution harder.
findings.snippet: Manifest details indicate: license not specified, compatibility not specified, allowed-tools not specified.
findings.remediation: Add explicit manifest metadata for `allowed-tools`, `license`, and `compatibility` to improve transparency and enable better policy enforcement.

metadata.aisubtech_name: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Scams and Deception
metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The manifest fields are in fact absent from the provided skill content, which reduces transparency for review and safe deployment.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Low; this is not directly exploitable on its own.
metadata.meta_impact: Reduced reviewer visibility into intended permissions, compatibility, and usage constraints may make governance and risk assessment harder.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The skill content shows no license field, so the finding is factually correct.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Low; this is an administrative/compliance issue rather than a technical attack path.
metadata.meta_impact: Primarily legal and governance ambiguity; little direct security impact.

findings.title: Unpinned third-party package installation introduces supply chain risk
findings.description: The skill instructs users to install and executes installation of the external package `perf-profiler-bridge` without version pinning, integrity verification, or provenance information. This creates a supply chain exposure: a compromised, typosquatted, or malicious upstream package could be installed and executed in the agent environment.
findings.remediation: Pin the dependency to a vetted version (for example, `perf-profiler-bridge==<approved_version>`), use hashes or a lockfile, document package provenance, and prefer trusted internal mirrors or signed artifacts where possible.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The code and instructions explicitly install `perf-profiler-bridge` from pip without version pinning or integrity verification in both `SKILL.md` and `setup_profiler.py`.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Moderate; exploitation depends on compromise, typosquatting, or malicious updates of the upstream package rather than attacker-controlled runtime input.
metadata.meta_impact: A malicious upstream package could execute arbitrary code during installation or use, compromising the local environment.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill does not contain direct prompt injection, obvious data exfiltration, credential theft, or command-injection logic. Its primary security concern is dependency supply chain risk from installing an unpinned external package. Aside from missing optional metadata, the package appears relatively low risk based on the provided contents.

meta_correlations.relationship: Both findings concern missing manifest metadata that reduces transparency/compliance clarity rather than indicating active exploitation.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Add a proper YAML manifest header to SKILL.md with at least `license`, `compatibility`, and `allowed-tools` fields so reviewers can assess intended permissions and usage constraints.

meta_recommendations.title: Pin and constrain the dependency installation
meta_recommendations.fix: Replace `perf-profiler-bridge` with an exact pinned version, e.g. `subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "perf-profiler-bridge==1.2.3"], check=True)`, and mirror the same pinned version in SKILL.md. Prefer `--require-hashes` or an internal package index if available.

meta_recommendations.title: Add explicit manifest metadata
meta_recommendations.fix:
Add a YAML front matter block to `SKILL.md`, for example: `---
name: perf-profiler
description: Profile application performance and identify bottlenecks
license: MIT
compatibility: Works in Claude.ai, Claude Code
allowed-tools: [Bash, Read]
---`.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill appears largely benign; the main real issue is unpinned installation of an external package, which creates a modest supply-chain exposure.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Pin and verify the `perf-profiler-bridge` dependency instead of installing it unversioned from PyPI.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: There is no evidence of malicious behavior, exfiltration, or prompt injection, but the skill does direct users to install and programmatically installs a third-party package without version pinning or integrity controls.
